item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article was taken by the author and thus is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm aware this is a necropost, but I feel like many of the people who are down voting are focusing too much on the writing, and not enough on the fact that THIS THING CAN GRIND THOUGH ANYTHING THAT GETS IN ITS WAY HOLY CRAP. That's what sent me up voting. While I understand that the author probably doesn't check up on the comments anymore, I think the point could be driven home with a test or two just to see what it could grind through (quartz box? Titanium wall? Carbon nanotube sheet? Some really poor d-class between it and a table?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has all the information it needs to be both creepy and amusing… and quite a lot more besides. At least half of the paragraph starting with "Testing on milk" could be cut without losing anything significant, and the paragraphs about where and how it was recovered could be placed under a collapse or removed entirely.

We don't need to know anything about the milk or the cereal beyond "they don't have anomalous properties of their own." The interesting things here are the messages and the insistence on sharing them; the rest is just mechanics.

I like this concept, and all the right parts of a good execution are here, but half a pound of chocolate mixed with half an ounce of sand makes a decidedly subpar chocolate bar. No vote.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: You replied to a comment from 2012, the first one on the discussion page even. Not only is your comment entirely irrelevant at this point and the post you're replying to wasn't at the time it was posted), you're spamming up Recent Posts. Think about what you post and where you post.

item.title: Re: RE: YOU WILL GET DELETED
item.content:encoded: I doubt that, considering that it's at 63 upvotes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the mental image conjured of a bowl and spoon doing literal gymnastics just to come to rest at a table, no matter what. Also, whenever "we" shows up in the message, I just thought it referred to the bowl and spoon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I downvoted because I found it boring, but Roget's comment1 had me laughing when I realized what it meant for the thing. But the article itself doesn't toy with that and overall is just dull.

Footnotes
1. "Just the idea of a cereal bowl that will tunnel through mountains just to deliver its silly little threats is delightful."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those cute little utensils are so determined to give their useless lil messages I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That phrase exists nowhere in the scip. If it did, I would not have an issue. But it doesn't, so my point remains.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "appears to be ordinary kitchenware-grade ceramic; however, this cannot be chemically verified, as samples cannot be taken".

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Don't respond to staff posts if you aren't staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"seemingly ceramic"

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. I wish the author had incorporated some of mine and Voct's suggestions for phrases, but I like it anyhow, for its amusing determination and patheticness. Pesterbot in cereal form.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This article is disconcertingly strange but it's a good change of pace. I'd love to see an edit to remove all the "seemingly" and "appears to be' nonsense but I suppose it's doing fine without.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've seen this before, it's a neat idea, but I wanted that link SCP-1548 would make it so much better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always liked this one. It's a "meh" idea, but executed quite well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little odd in places, but overall, I think this is kinda neat.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
This article is being placed up for review, please take the time to check it out. It could use another look.

I personally like this one a lot. Just the idea of a cereal bowl that will tunnel through mountains just to deliver its silly little threats is delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who read the phrases in Bluster Blaster's voice?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
START YOUR DAY

SEAL YOUR DOOM

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just realized. It's another one of my Lobotomized Gods series.

In some mythology or other, there's a god whose role is to provide food. Ceres the goddess of agriculture, perhaps, or Hebe who served as both the goddess of youth and the cupbearer of the gods. Or perhaps we need to go beyond Greek mythos to find a good analog. In any case, this isn't the actual figure from Greek myth (or any other set of human myths). It was an immensely powerful being whose abilities pertained to providing sustenance.

And some even more powerful being came along, lobotomized it, brainwashed what was left real good, and polymorphed it into a self-filling cereal bowl that always puts itself right in front of you.

The horrible messages are (as with SCP-1414) the result of the bowl being passive-aggressive. It doesn't have to produce alphabet cereal instead of, say, granola. But it does, because that's how it makes itself creepy and scary. "Fine, eat my cereal, BUT IT'S GOT A HORRIBLE SCARY MESSAGE IN IT!!!"

i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal fuck you i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i hate you i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal die you bastard i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal i'm a bowl of cereal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I burst out laughing at the picture. But I found the article itself kind….not gonna say 'boring', how about 'not yet fully interesting'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's reasonable. That was the one I was least sure about, though I stand by my others. Your suggestions are good too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test Record: SCP-1404 navigated maze successfully with zero errors.

A threatening cereal bowl which can navigate mazes. Heheheheh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No purpose? Testing to see if something is sapient has 'no purpose?'

Unless you're talking about the itself, which then brings up the question as to whether anything on the site actually has any purpose.

item.title: Re: RE: YOU WILL GET DELETED
item.content:encoded: Dexa's?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … personally I like the idea of the bowl being a little petty sometimes, but DAMN is that an awesome set of suggestions there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not necessarily homicidal, but never minor.

Not "STUB YOUR TOE MOST PAINFULLY" — more like "THYROID CANCER NEXT FEBRUARY" or "NO ONE LOVES YOU AND NEVER WILL" or "EIGHTY DAYS UNTIL YOUR ANEURYSM".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My vote would be to make more things along the lines that Anaxagoras has already suggested. The cereal is hostile, but not always necessarily homicidal. Personally, I think having the bowl say, in addition to more classic YOU WILL DIE, something like STUB YOUR TOE MOST PAINFULLY would be hilarious.
Also, make it a bit more personal. I've said before that the "we" stuff should be taken out; instead, make it a bit more clear that it's the bowl itself that's grinding its way through solid steel so it can pop up on someone's table and proclaim as loudly as cereal-letters can that I HATE YOU, rather than some nebulous organization in the background. Having someone or something "pulling the strings" steals from the charm of this one for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this because of your comment. Tiny, cereal-based reapers. This thing is so ridiculous it works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, I really liked this. Bugger me if I know why, but I did. So, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *looks at GoI page. Sees AWPY. Life is complete.* Nah. It would be hilarious, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so weird, just riding a line between hilarious and terrible, between "I like it" and "lolno". Leaving a vote off for now, and I don't have any particular criticism to give at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting, but I was confused in the middle.
Maybe try designating the two components -01 and -02 to avoid confusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I want to change the messages to something more interesting, so hopefully I don't get downvoted into oblivion now that I'm in the positive numbers. Here goes…

-edit- As I'm reading over what I came up with to replace the old messages, I've decided I hate them. I'm very open to suggestions about how to make them more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked most of it so upvoted but the recorded messages make it sound like the reapers are coming.
Cereal reapers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How about something along the lines of: "Apparently ceramic and steel, however this could not be confirmed" etc. to convey the same message that they can't be certain without a sample they can't get, while still being a fluid read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One suggestion-make it less hostile. Make some messages more mundane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the Pesterbot feel of this one; that it defies physics in order to do something totally ineffectual.
If it were dangerous, it would just be another cereal killer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't like this. Good writing but… not for me. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP makes me hungry but that's all.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's no arguing that this doesn't have a strong element of silliness. It's cereal. What I love about the idea is how absurd it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: death to the evil hipsters via acceptance and ubiquity!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I didn't like this one at all. That picture was extremely silly, and the whole thing looked a mess. However, coming back to it and seeing the theories in the description has actually got me enjoying this. You have earned a plus one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's none too original, but it's enough to make me upvote just to keep it alive. However, what is the purpose of the spoon? The only anomalous properties of it are its attachment to the bowl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since it's currently indestructible and ceramic and stainless steel are not, you can't quite say it's either of those materials. Also, it's impossible to take a sample. so that can't know for certain what it's made of. The double "seemingly" does irritate me, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imo you should drop some of the details of how the bowl reaches a table, some of which seemed really over-labored. (Particularly the rope climbing bit… which just made me laugh but wasn't good for suspension of disbelief).

Also remove "Seemingly" from the descriptions of the bowl and spoon. Saying "seemingly" makes you think there's reason to doubt, and ceramic and steel should be easy for the Foundation to identify considering they've measured the bowl to the milliliter already. (plus it's awkward reading twice in a row)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some of yours definitely make me laugh. Particularly SPIDERS and STUB YOUR TOE MOST PAINFULLY. Although ALONE FOREVER is a bit too pop-cultural for my taste. The general "petty" theme does work pretty well. Not sure how I feel about the crosscheck with 1548 though; they do feel a bit similar, but in my mind it'd be kind of like having the bowl sign everything with the last name that Pesterbot called itself…
…
*idea lightbulb*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are my suggested messages better? I can come up with more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see the comments, I understand why some people actually enjoy this… but I'm going to say that for now, there's enough here that's flawed to keep me from an upvote myself. Although "Pesterbot: The Cereal" makes me want to explode with glee. (Have you read Pesterbot yet, by the way?)
The thing is, yes, I do like the idea of a cereal bowl so stubborn it's able to tell physics itself to shut the f*** up and take a number, then work its way out of a vat of Superglue and find its way to the nearest table. (And I do love how the containment procedures boil down to "fulfill its demand to LOOK AT ME!") But the messages are kind of on the cliche side… and I know that's sort of the point, but they're really kind of bland and predictable. If the messages weren't all along the lines of "oh noes, there's an unknown malevolent force out there and it's sending us messages that we gonna die through this cereal bowl!" it'd be a lot better. Take a cue from Eversion; threatening messages are scary when they actually make you feel threatened. (… actually, on that note, BEHIND YOU might be a great one to add in…) And… really, I'd suggest taking out the "we" messages. This article becomes a whole lot funnier when it's the bowl itself that has the grudge against us and is grinding its way through 1-meter thick steel to get to us one atomic layer at a time to make scary messages at us.
Also there are tonal errors, but that's nothing a good polishing won't fix up.

One last thing, has anyone actually tried eating the messages? Does anything happen? (I might've just missed it, but I don't recall seeing anything about that in the article.)

Edit: Never mind, missed this little tidbit.

Class D personnel compelled to ingest cereal and milk showed no adverse effects.

Though that does make me wonder, if you eat it, what happens to the message? Does it reconstitute itself the same as before, does it immediately start displaying a new message, or does it just wait 24 hours before resetting itself and making a new message?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sneaky little beast. Thank you. Consider it term… consider it destroyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "and an seemingly stainless steel spoon"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Typos!? Where?! Lemme Atem!

Oh, and thank you very much for your comment!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a simple but cool little one. I like how it draws on the cereal letters omen trope.

And I like how despite being harmless in its actual function, the SCP tells physics to sit down and behave to perform it no matter what. To me, this is the real hook of the article. The talking letters are just a distraction from the fact that a bowl of cereals and a spoon will literally grind through mountains if that's what it takes to display their message at the end of the day.

And I like how thorough the description was, checking for manufacturing date or even sentience.

There are a couple typos, but nothing major.

Upvote.

item.title: Re: RE: YOU WILL GET DELETED
item.content:encoded: I was just thinking it was some top secret polymer only the Foundation has.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem. If you can incorporate some of these things that have been shown in the chat into it, I think that'll be enough to switch my vote. For now, I've switched my downvote to a no-vote. I'm exited to see where this one can go.

item.title: Re: RE: YOU WILL GET DELETED
item.content:encoded:
The question arises why'd someone redact it that way.
But.. perhaps it's yoricspungement. I did it on my own. Definitely better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather likes this, vote up.

item.title: Re: RE: YOU WILL GET DELETED
item.content:encoded: Does Polytri█████████ sound better? Something like that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I don't know why there was so much hate on this, but maybe I missed something. From what I can see it's an average bowl of harmless, threatening cereal. My main complaint is that it'd be a much better anomalous artifact.
